About me

A practical academic profile combining software development, optimization, experimentation, and intelligent systems.

Optimization

Routing and logistics

I work on algorithmic methods for complex routing, planning, and operational decision problems.

AI & Data

Intelligent methods

I use clustering, search, data analysis, and evaluation methods to build useful intelligent systems.

Software

Implementation mindset

I enjoy transforming research ideas into modular, testable, and understandable software prototypes.

Selected projects

A general overview of my academic and technical work, with the master thesis presented as one major project.

01
Master Thesis Project

Hybrid K-Means Clustering and Tabu Search for EVRP

Developed a hybrid optimization approach for the Electric Vehicle Routing Problem. The project combines K-Means clustering, greedy construction, energy-feasibility repair, charging-station handling, and Tabu Search improvement.

Python EVRP K-Means Tabu Search Benchmarking
Open Master Thesis PDF
02
Software Development

Laravel internship management application

Designed and developed a web application during a full-stack development internship, contributing to requirements understanding, implementation, testing, usability, and performance improvement.

Laravel PHP JavaScript HTML/CSS
03
Research Direction

AI-powered decision support for social good

Research interest in building AI-assisted software systems that support planning and decision-making in transportation, public services, logistics, and resource-constrained contexts.

AI for Social Good Decision Support Evaluation
04
Research Practice

Experimental benchmarking and academic writing

Prepared research reports using LaTeX, structured results through tables and figures, and evaluated algorithmic behavior using solution quality, convergence, runtime, and comparative metrics.

LaTeX Reports Figures Analysis

Research approach

The way I usually move from a research idea to a working and evaluated prototype.

01 · UnderstandStudy the problem, constraints, literature, and expected outputs.
02 · ModelTranslate the problem into data structures, rules, and algorithmic components.
03 · ImplementBuild modular code, test feasibility, debug, and generate reproducible outputs.
04 · EvaluateRun experiments, compare results, analyze performance, and communicate findings.
